name: 🚀 Cosmos
description: Space missions, planetary observations and satellites — a full bronze → silver → gold pipeline that showcases multiple merge strategies, layered transforms, quality tests, table-level documentation and a cron schedule.

pipelines.name: missions
pipelines.description: 200 synthetic rocket missions across six agencies and five planetary targets — full_refresh strategy.

table_metadata.description: Rocket missions registry. One row per mission, regenerated in full every run (full_refresh).

column_descriptions.mission_id: Public mission identifier in the form M-0001.
column_descriptions.program: Space agency that flew the mission (SpaceX, NASA, ESA, ...).

pipelines.name: observations
pipelines.description: Telescope observations — APPEND_ONLY strategy. Each run adds new observation rows; nothing is rewritten.

table_metadata.description: Telescope observations of planetary bodies. Append-only: every run extends the history.

column_descriptions.observatory: Where the observation was taken (Mauna Kea, Paranal, Hubble, JWST).

pipelines.name: satellites
pipelines.description: 120 satellites in current operation grouped by type and operator.

table_metadata.description: Active satellites inventory. Operator + orbit altitude + launch date.

pipelines.name: missions_enriched
pipelines.description: Missions joined with derived mission_type and decade — uses ref() to read bronze.

pipelines.name: observations_daily
pipelines.description: Observations aggregated to one row per day-planet — uses ref() to read the append-only bronze.

pipelines.name: success_by_program
pipelines.description: Mission success rate per agency. Cron-scheduled hourly for live dashboards.

pipelines.name: satellite_summary
pipelines.description: Satellites grouped by type and operator.

quality_tests.name: missions_id_not_null
quality_tests.description: Every mission must have a mission_id — primary-key completeness.
